spec_ids: UNS N07718; AMS 5662; AMS 5664; W.Nr 2.4668; DIN NiCr19Fe19NbMo3
product_forms: wrought; sheet; rings; casings; fasteners; powder
heat_treatment_summary: Solution treat 1850-1900F, then age at 1450F for 6-8 h and air cool; AM parts are precipitation hardened after build.
joining_fabrication_notes: Good weldability and resistance to post-weld cracking; AM parts can be machined, welded, shot-peened, polished and coated.
operating_temperature_band: Cryogenic service noted; EOS AM sheet highlights good tensile/fatigue/creep/rupture strength up to ~700C / 1290F.
application_notes: Gas turbine components; instrumentation parts; rocket and power-industry parts.
known_watch_outs: Requires precipitation hardening; manage distortion/cracking risk in AM builds and post-build heat treatment.
match_guardrails: Do not use when Co is dominant or when chemistry drifts strongly toward corrosion-first Ni-Mo alloys rather than age-hardened Ni superalloys.
provenance_note: Special Metals bulletin + EOS AM data sheet

nominal_composition_text: Ni 58 min, Cr 20-23, Mo 8-10, Nb+Ta 3.15-4.15, Fe max 5
product_forms: wrought; plate; welded fabrications; corrosion-service components
heat_treatment_summary: Strength comes from Mo and Nb in the Ni-Cr matrix; precipitation-hardening treatments are not required.
joining_fabrication_notes: Excellent fabricability including joining; filler metals based on 625 are used without post-weld heat treatment to maintain strength and ductility.
operating_temperature_band: Service temperatures from cryogenic to about 1800F / 982C are noted in the bulletin.
application_notes: Sea-water and severe-corrosion service; high-strength ductile welded fabrications.
known_watch_outs: Usually a corrosion/service analogue rather than the best creep-first hot-section analogue; avoid over-claiming AM route support from this starter row.
match_guardrails: Do not force-match to gamma-prime-style age-hardened Ni-superalloy candidates where IN718/282-like logic is a better fit.

nominal_composition_text: Ni balance, Cr 20, Co 10, Mo 8.5, Ti 2.1, Al 1.5, Fe max 1.5
product_forms: sheet; plate; rings; closed-die forgings; bar; heavy plate blanks; powder
heat_treatment_summary: Typical solution anneal 2050-2100F; standard age-hardening treatment 1850F / 2 h / AC + 1450F / 8 h / AC. EOS notes as-built parts need solution + aging to develop high-temperature strength.
joining_fabrication_notes: Designed for strong fabricability and weldability relative to creep-capable superalloys; AM feedstock exists for EOS DMLS.
operating_temperature_band: Haynes positions it for high-temperature structural use; EOS positions it for aerospace, rocket engine, turbomachinery and gas-turbine parts.
application_notes: Combustors; turbine and exhaust sections; nozzle components; rocket engine and gas turbine parts.
known_watch_outs: Do not treat as a no-heat-treatment alloy; high-temperature strength depends on proper solution + age route.
match_guardrails: Do not use when the candidate lacks a Ni base or when chemistry is much closer to corrosion-first Ni-Mo systems than precipitation-strengthened superalloys.
provenance_note: Haynes brochure + EOS AM data sheet

spec_ids: UNS R56400; AMS 4928; AMS 4965; AMS 4967; ASTM B348; ASTM F1472; ASTM F2924; ASTM F3302
nominal_composition_text: Ti balance, Al 5.5-6.75, V 3.5-4.5 (Grade 5 Ti6Al4V chemistry class)
heat_treatment_summary: Anneal 1275-1400F for 0.5-2 h; stress relieve 1000-1200F for 1-8 h; solution treat 1675-1750F for 1 h, water quench; age 975-1025F for 4-8 h. EOS recommends heat treatment to reduce internal stresses and increase ductility.
joining_fabrication_notes: Most widely used titanium grade; AM parts can be machined, shot-peened and polished; forging and wrought routes are standard.
operating_temperature_band: Used from cryogenic temperatures to about 800F / 427C in the ATI sheet; lightweight aerospace structural alloy.
application_notes: Compressor blades, discs and rings; airframe components; pressure vessels; rocket engine cases; rotor hubs; fasteners.
known_watch_outs: AM parts are anisotropic and generally need stress-relief / post-treatment; control oxygen/nitrogen/hydrogen pickup during processing.
match_guardrails: Do not use for Ni/Co hot-section analogues just because high strength is needed; this is a lightweight Ti structural analogue.
provenance_note: ATI wrought data sheet + EOS AM data sheet

spec_ids: AMS 4930; AMS 4931; AMS 4996; ASTM F136; ASTM F3001; ASTM F3302
nominal_composition_text: Ti-6Al-4V ELI class; same Ti/Al/V family as Ti64 with lower oxygen, carbon and iron than standard Grade 5
composition_vector_json: {"Ti": "balance", "Al": "Ti-6Al-4V class", "V": "Ti-6Al-4V class", "interstitials": "ELI / lower O,C,Fe than Grade 5"}
heat_treatment_summary: Normally used annealed in ATI sheet: anneal 1300-1350F for 1-8 h, air cool; stress relieve 900-1200F for 1-4 h. EOS Grade 23 positioning supports HIP-centric AM users.
joining_fabrication_notes: Higher-purity Ti64 variant with better formability and weld toughness than standard Grade 5; inert-gas protection is needed in fusion welding.
operating_temperature_band: Preferred for some cryogenic and aerospace uses; EOS Grade 23 positioning supports additive workflows where HIP is a standard post-treatment.
application_notes: Cryogenic-capable aerospace components; high-toughness Ti64 ELI use cases; medical and marine/cryogenic overlap exists in source literature.
known_watch_outs: Keep recipe claims cautious unless route/source is explicitly Grade 23-backed; preserve low-interstitial processing discipline.
match_guardrails: Do not force-match to standard Grade 5 if toughness / low-interstitial behavior is the real reason the candidate is attractive; keep ELI as a distinct analogue.
provenance_note: ATI Grade 23 sheet + EOS Grade 23 AM sheet

nominal_composition_text: Co balance, Ni 22, Cr 22, W 14, Fe max 3, La 0.03
heat_treatment_summary: Usually furnished solution heat treated; normally 2125-2175F and rapidly cooled or water quenched for optimal properties.
joining_fabrication_notes: Readily fabricated and formed by conventional techniques; good forming and welding characteristics are highlighted by Haynes.
operating_temperature_band: Very good oxidation resistance for prolonged exposures up to 2000F / 1095C is highlighted in the brochure.
application_notes: Combustion cans; transition ducts; afterburner components; other aerospace gas-turbine hot-section fabrications.
known_watch_outs: High Co/W burden will likely worsen sustainability and supply-risk scores; use conventional-fabrication route logic unless trusted AM evidence is added later.
match_guardrails: Do not use as a Ni-superalloy analogue just because Ni is present; keep Co-dominant chemistry as the decisive family cue.

nominal_composition_text: Mo base with about 0.5% Ti, 0.08% Zr and 0.01-0.04% C
heat_treatment_summary: Use specialist refractory processing logic rather than generic superalloy heat-treatment windows; treat as a powder-metallurgy / refractory-working analogue in v1.
joining_fabrication_notes: Plansee positions TZM as stronger and harder than pure molybdenum over a wide temperature range due to fine carbide additions.
operating_temperature_band: High-temperature refractory use; use as a refractory-family anchor rather than as a direct aircraft-certified alloy recipe source.
application_notes: Refractory high-temperature tooling and hot-zone style anchor; useful as a Mo-Ti-Zr refractory analogue.
known_watch_outs: Avoid over-claiming aerospace certification or AM readiness from this starter row; oxidation/protective-environment logic needs specialist treatment.
match_guardrails: Do not use when Ni/Co-based hot-section fabrication logic is required; this row is for refractory-family matching and specialist route generation.

spec_ids: ASTM B652; ASTM B654; ASTM B655
nominal_composition_text: Nb-10Hf-1Ti refractory alloy (niobium base with hafnium and titanium)
product_forms: sheet; rod; wire; powder/AM study context
heat_treatment_summary: Starter row does not fix a single certified heat-treatment window; Elmet AM paper discusses LPBF parts followed by HIP, coating and further heat treatment to simulate service conditions.
joining_fabrication_notes: Materion highlights good weldability and product availability in sheet/rod/wire; Elmet shows LPBF feasibility for C103 in an aerospace context.
operating_temperature_band: Elmet notes historic space-propulsion use around operating temperatures near 1300C for thruster-nozzle environments.
application_notes: Rocket nozzles; satellites; launch vehicles; jet-engine afterburner flap sections; high-temperature aerospace components.
known_watch_outs: High-temperature oxidizing service typically needs a protective coating such as R512E; HIP/coating can reduce AM strength versus wrought specifications.
match_guardrails: Do not treat as a generic Mo- or W-based refractory analogue; keep Nb-Hf-Ti chemistry and coating need explicit in recipe generation.
provenance_note: Materion product page + Elmet AM paper
